Legal.privacyLead: This document explains how PROVIN (“we”, “the service”) processes personal data, what cookies are used, and how you can consent to or decline non-essential analytics and first-touch (UTM) storage.

sections.title: 1. Controller
sections.body: The owner of the PROVIN service is responsible for processing personal data. Contact details are in the site footer (email, WhatsApp). You may request full controller details through those channels.

sections.title: 2. Data we process
sections.body: For orders: name, email, phone, VIN, listing URL, free-text message, and payment metadata (processed by Stripe under its policy). For the website: server logs (e.g. IP address, request time) may be used for security and reliability.

sections.title: 3. Purposes and legal basis
sections.body: Contract and order fulfilment (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)). Legitimate interests: security and troubleshooting (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)). Analytics and first-touch (UTM) storage — only after your voluntary consent via the cookie banner (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a)).

sections.title: 4. Cookies and essential features
sections.body: Essential features (e.g. language preference, session, security) may use cookies or similar technologies without separate consent where strictly necessary to provide the service. A detailed list can be provided on request.

sections.title: 5. Analytics and attribution (after consent)
sections.body: If you accept “analytics” in the banner, we may store limited information about your first visit: page path, HTTP referrer when available, and marketing parameters from the URL (UTM: source, medium, campaign, etc.). The goal is to understand traffic sources (e.g. TikTok, YouTube, search) without selling data.

sections.title: 6. Third parties
sections.body: Payments: Stripe. Hosting: your chosen provider (e.g. Vercel). Third-party analytics (e.g. Google Analytics) may be added in the future — only with separate consent and an update to this policy.

sections.title: 7. Retention
sections.body: Order and customer data are kept as long as needed to provide the service and meet legal obligations. Analytics/attribution cookies are limited in duration (typically up to 180 days) unless stated otherwise.

sections.title: 8. Your rights
sections.body: Under the GDPR you may access, rectify, erase, restrict processing, object, and request data portability where applicable. You may lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.

sections.title: 9. Changes
sections.body: We may update this policy. Material changes will be indicated on the site or via the cookie banner where appropriate.

Cookies.bannerText: We use essential cookies for the site to work. If you agree, we can also store anonymous information about your first page and marketing link parameters (UTM) to understand where visitors come from.
